name: ServiceNow, Inc.

profile.description: ServiceNow provides a cloud-based platform for enterprise digital workflows, automating IT service management, employee experience, customer service, and security operations. The company is positioning itself as the enterprise AI operating system for the Agentic AI era.

innovation.label: STRONG
innovation.analysis: Track Record (3/3): ServiceNow delivered Q4 2025 subscription revenue of $3.47B (21% YoY growth), 244 transactions over $1M in net new ACV (40% YoY growth), and 603 customers with $5M+ ACV [1]. The company is successfully transforming from ITSM vendor to enterprise AI platform. AI Deployment (2/2): ServiceNow's Kokai AI platform has near-100% client adoption, generating $355M in annualized value from its own agentic AI capabilities, with 20%+ productivity gains across customer, HR, and IT support [2]. The AI Control Tower provides centralized AI agent governance. Market Share (2/2): ServiceNow dominates enterprise workflow automation with 98% renewal rates and 25% cRPO growth, leading the ITSM category and expanding into security, HR, and customer workflows [1]. Pipeline (1/2): Armis acquisition (security) and Veza acquisition (identity) expected to close in 2026, tripling the security market opportunity; $5B share buyback authorized [1]. R&D Intensity (1/1): Heavy R&D investment with 97% of standard software now AI-generated internally, demonstrating best-in-class engineering productivity [2].

inspiration.label: STRONG
inspiration.analysis: Mission Floor (3/4): ServiceNow's purpose of making the world of work, work better for people is specific and operational, directly connecting technology to human productivity and job satisfaction. McDermott's vision of AI replacing 'soul-crushing jobs' rather than all jobs adds depth [2]. Community Impact (1/2): ServiceNow employs 29,187 people and has created ServiceNow University and the AI Institute to democratize learning, aiming to create 'a renaissance of learning and knowledge where people can rise up' [2]. Employee Retention (2/2): Strong talent attraction — Miku Jha left Google Cloud AI for ServiceNow citing 'bold product vision paired with open and transparent collaboration.' The culture emphasizes ownership, speed, and experimentation with career growth [2]. Insider Buying (1/2): CEO McDermott has significant equity exposure; $5B share buyback authorization and $2B accelerated repurchase demonstrate institutional conviction [1].

infrastructure.label: DEVELOPING
infrastructure.analysis: ServiceNow's financials reflect high-growth SaaS dynamics. D/E of 18.5% is very low, well below the 70% target [4]. ROE of 15.5% meets the 15% target. Revenue growth of 20.7% YoY is excellent, above the 10% target [1]. Forward P/E of 20.8x is near the 20x target, reasonable for 20%+ growth. Profit margin of 13.2% is below the 20% target but improving with scale. FY2026 operating margin guided to ~32% (non-GAAP), FCF margin to ~36% [1]. The forward PEG of 1.06x is near the 1.0x target, suggesting fair value for the growth rate. Liquidity floor easily cleared: 18.3M avg vol x $104.65 = $1.9B daily [4].

ai_resilience.label: LOW RISK
ai_resilience.analysis: AI Risk Protection (8/9): ServiceNow is not just AI-resilient — it is the enterprise AI operating system. CEO McDermott positions the company as the only platform that can 'orchestrate AI, data, and workflows on a single platform' [2]. The Kokai AI platform has near-100% client adoption, generating $355M in annualized value, while the AI Control Tower provides governance for any AI agent across the enterprise. AI is 'devouring' low-value SaaS competitors, consolidating workflow spending onto ServiceNow's platform [2]. The 98% renewal rate and 40% growth in $1M+ deals demonstrate that customers are deepening AI-driven commitments. AI Opportunity (1/1): Agentic AI is ServiceNow's core growth driver — McDermott projects AI agents will save workers 2.5 days per week in 2026, up from 1 day in 2025, creating massive productivity-driven demand expansion [2].

infinite_game.summary: ServiceNow under CEO McDermott plays an aggressive infinite game — investing heavily in AI platform capabilities, acquiring strategically (Armis, Veza, Moveworks), and building a learning culture that positions the company for decades of enterprise digital transformation. The 98% renewal rate, 25% cRPO growth, and $5B buyback authorization demonstrate both customer trust and long-term conviction.

just_cause.analysis: Making the world of work, work better for people is a genuine and validated cause [2]. McDermott's framing of AI as replacing 'soul-crushing jobs' while empowering human creativity is compelling and specific. The ServiceNow University and AI Institute demonstrate commitment to workforce development beyond profit.

trusting_teams.analysis: ServiceNow's culture of 'ownership and speed' with experimentation, career growth, and budget access creates high-trust teams [2]. Attracting talent from Google Cloud, having a CPO who doubles as Chief AI Enablement Officer, and generating 97% of standard software through AI internally demonstrate best-in-class team capability and trust.

worthy_rivals.analysis: ServiceNow openly engages with Salesforce (Marc Benioff) in a public competitive dialogue about the future of enterprise AI, using the rivalry to sharpen its platform positioning [2]. Partnerships with Microsoft, Google, Oracle, and NVIDIA show the company selectively collaborates with technology rivals.

existential_flexibility.analysis: ServiceNow has expanded from ITSM to HR, customer workflows, security, and now agentic AI — each extension building on the core platform [1]. The Armis and Veza acquisitions triple the security market opportunity. However, the company's success is deeply tied to enterprise IT workflow automation; a paradigm shift away from workflow-centric models could pose challenges.

courage_to_lead.analysis: McDermott's bold statements — calling for '20th century org charts to be obliterated' and positioning ServiceNow as the enterprise AI OS — demonstrate visionary courage [2]. The company invests heavily in AI even when it cannibalizes existing revenue streams (slowing hiring in 'soul-crushing' roles while growing the platform).

investment_thesis: ServiceNow is the premier enterprise AI platform play, delivering 21% subscription revenue growth with 98% renewal rates and rapidly expanding deal sizes (244 transactions over $1M in Q4, up 40% YoY) [1]. The company is positioned at the intersection of every major enterprise technology trend — AI workflow automation, agentic AI, security operations, and digital transformation. FY2026 guidance of 19.5-20% subscription revenue growth with expanding margins (32% operating, 36% FCF) demonstrates durable growth with improving economics [1]. The Kokai AI platform's near-100% adoption and $355M in annualized AI value show that ServiceNow is not just selling AI but deploying it profitably [2]. At 20.8x forward P/E with a 1.06x PEG ratio, the valuation is reasonable for a 20%+ growth compounder. Strategic acquisitions (Armis tripling security TAM) and the $5B buyback provide additional growth vectors and shareholder returns [1].
thesis_continued: The bear case centers on valuation premium and competitive threats. At 62.7x trailing P/E, the stock requires sustained 20%+ growth to justify its multiple — any deceleration would trigger significant multiple compression [4]. Salesforce is aggressively competing in ITSM with agentic AI capabilities, and the head-to-head battle could pressure pricing and market share [2]. The Moveworks acquisition adds ~1 point to subscription growth, raising questions about organic growth durability. Profit margin of 13.2% GAAP is still modest despite premium pricing, and the Armis acquisition could introduce integration risk. ServiceNow's rapid AI deployment creates dependency risk — if enterprise AI adoption slows or the promised productivity gains (2.5 days saved per week) don't materialize, growth expectations could reset sharply downward [2].
key_catalysts: Agentic AI platform adoption accelerating — $355M annualized AI value, near-100% Kokai adoption, projected to save workers 2.5 days/week in 2026 [2]
Armis acquisition closing H2 2026, tripling security market opportunity and creating a unified security-operations stack [1]
FY2026 subscription revenue guidance of $15.5B+ (19.5-20% growth) with expanding operating margins to 32% and FCF margins to 36% [1]
key_risks: Premium trailing P/E of 62.7x requires sustained 20%+ growth — any deceleration would trigger significant multiple compression [4]
Intensifying competition from Salesforce in ITSM and agentic AI could pressure pricing and win rates in key enterprise accounts [2]
AI productivity gains (2.5 days saved per week) must materialize at scale to justify enterprise spending increases — if ROI disappoints, deal velocity could slow [2]
sources: ServiceNow Q4 2025 & FY2025 Results — $3.47B subscription revenue (21% YoY), 244 $1M+ deals, 98% renewal, $5B buyback, Armis/Veza acquisitions, January 2026
Cloud Wars, Computer Weekly, McKinsey — CEO McDermott AI vision, Kokai platform, agentic AI strategy, Knowledge 2025 conference, 2025-2026
ServiceNow Global Impact — ESG initiatives, sustainability commitments
